content:
Here is the weekly ASX X (Twitter) thread for the week of 2026-07-13:

Post 1
📈 ASX 200 closed at 8,547 pts last week. The tension: US tariff noise is fading but domestic growth data is softening. Mixed stance — global tailwinds vs local headwinds. Watch 8,600 as the line in the sand.
#ASX #ausbiz #macro

Post 2
🏦 Banks face margin squeeze scrutiny as RBA cut expectations reprice. ⛏️ Miners get a read on Chinese demand via fresh trade data. Two sectors, two catalysts — diverging paths this week.
#ASX #banking #mining #RBA

Post 3
🔺 REA Group reports this week. With Australian property listings up 12% year-on-year, any guidance upgrade on volumes could be the catalyst that breaks the stock to new highs.
#ASX #property #ausbiz

Post 4
S&P 500 sits near 5,900 pts after shrugging off June CPI. If US earnings season opens strong this week, risk appetite flows through to ASX materials and tech by Thursday open.
#ASX #ausbiz #SP500 #globalmarkets

Post 5
RBA minutes drop Tuesday. Markets price one more 25bps cut by year-end. Hawkish tone crushes rate-sensitive REITs and banks. Dovish tone lifts them hard. Which side are you on — one cut or done?
#ASX #RBA #ausbiz #interestrates

Post 1
ASX 200 barely moved Monday — up 2.50pts to 8,808.50 — but the flat index masked real divergence underneath. IT fell 2.49%, energy rose 0.66%. Still 4.29% off its 52-week high. #ASX #ausbiz

Post 2
📉 IT dropped 2.49%, Utilities shed 1.63%. On the other side, Telcos +0.89%, Consumer Discretionary +0.86%, Financials +0.67%. Rotation into defensives and yield plays dominated Monday's session. #ASX #ausbiz

Post 3
🛢️ Ampol surged 4.17% to AUD 36.75, with Viva Energy adding 3.56% to AUD 2.33. Fuel retailers led the energy sector's 0.66% gain — a rare bright spot in an otherwise cautious session. #energy #ASX

Post 4
🪙 Gold at AUD 5,860.26/oz with AUD/USD at 0.6929. A softer local currency keeps gold elevated in AUD terms — supportive for local producers even as the Materials sector dipped 0.65%. #gold #mining #ASX

Post 5
Watch the AUD/USD 0.6929 handle. If the USD strengthens this week, AUD gold stays bid above AUD 5,800/oz — a tailwind for producers. Does Materials bounce or stay under pressure? Drop your call. #ASX #gold #ausbiz
